pattern: Group reserved seats by row, then use bitmask logic to check the three possible four-seat blocks per row; unaffected rows each fit two groups.
hints: Rows with no reservations can always fit two groups (seats 2-5 and 6-9); only process rows that appear in reservedSeats.
For each affected row, represent the reserved inner seats (2-9) as a bitmask and check whether any of the three blocks (2-5, 4-7, 6-9) has all four seats free.

Count blocked block pairs using the bitmask: if left block 2-5 is free use it; else if right block 6-9 is free use it; the middle block 4-7 can only be used if neither side was taken—total is at most 2 per row.

edge_inputs.description: single row fully reserved in middle
left and right blocks both free
n=1 row, seat 1 and 10 reserved only
n very large with few reservations
center block 4-7 reserved by one seat blocks both left and middle
only seats 1 and 10 taken, both blocks available
all three blocks partially blocked, zero groups

stress.description: n=10^9 rows, 10000 random reservations spread across 1000 rows
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 10**9
    reserved = set()
    for _ in range(size):
        r = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        s = rng.randint(1, 10)
        reserved.add((r, s))
    return [n, [list(x) for x in reserved]]
